name: Alaskan Fish Bake
recipeInstructions:
1. Gently boil shredded carrot, chopped celery, and onion in water with the chicken bouillon cube, covered, for 10 minutes. Stir in the long-grain rice and margarine. Simmer, covered, for an additional 20 minutes (not all liquid will be absorbed).
2. Combine flour, garlic salt, dill weed, and onion powder on waxed paper. Spread mayonnaise on both sides of each salmon steak. Dip the steaks into the flour mixture.
3. Lightly brown the seasoned salmon steaks in an ungreased skillet over medium heat.
4. Pour the unstrained rice mixture into a shallow baking dish, approximately 11x7 inches. Place the browned salmon steaks on top of the rice mixture.
5. Bake, uncovered, in a preheated hot oven (400°F) for 20-25 minutes or until the fish is cooked through. Let stand for 5 minutes before serving.
